title: Slim Arm Side Table
summary: A waterfall-style slim side table from one 1x10 — four boards in a C-shape that slides against a sofa arm, stiffened by hidden 1x4 ribs inside.
description:
This is a one-board project with a furniture-store silhouette: a 1x10 folded into a slim C-shaped table — top, two sides, and a low shelf — that snugs up against a sofa arm to hold the phone, the mug, and the book the phone replaced. Total lumber: one 1x10 and a stick of 1x4.

The design's quiet cleverness is the 1x4 ribs. Four 3/4" panels joined at their edges would rack into a parallelogram the first time the table gets dragged across a rug — so three 1x4s attach inside, against the top and sides, fastened on every available face. They're invisible in use and they're most of the table's stiffness; the source is emphatic about attaching them on ALL available sides, and that emphasis is the plan's structure talking.

Joinery is a menu by honesty: pocket holes (3/4" setting, 1-1/4" screws) are strongest and mostly hidden; 2" screws or brads with glue work but are weaker and leave holes to fill before finishing. Whatever fastens it, GLUE every joint — in a piece this light the glue is doing the real work.

Sand to 120, fill anything visible, and finish. At about 22" tall and under 10" wide it fits where actual side tables don't — which is the entire pitch.

tools.note: Seven straight crosscuts. Circular saw with a square works fine.
3/4" setting — the strongest and cleanest of the three fastening options.
The quick option — with glue, and with filler at finish time.

materials.name: 1x10, 8 ft
materials.note: The whole visible table. Pick a flat one — every face shows.

materials.name: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or 2" screws, or 1-1/4" brads
Wood glue
materials.note: Every joint. The glue is the structure.

materials.name: Wood filler, sandpaper, and stain or paint

cutList.note: Hidden inside — attached on every available face.

steps.title: Join the C
steps.body:
Attach the two sides under the top (the top overhangs slightly at each end) and fit the shelf between the sides near the bottom. Pocket holes on the 3/4" setting with 1-1/4" screws are the strong, hidden choice; 2" screws or brads with glue also work if you'll fill the holes at finishing.

Glue every joint regardless of fastener — and square the assembly by diagonals before anything cures.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or 2" screws, or 1-1/4" brads

steps.title: Rib it, then finish
steps.body:
Fit the three 1x4 ribs inside — against the underside of the top and the inside faces of the sides — with glue and brads on EVERY face each rib touches. The ribs are the anti-rack structure; a rib tacked on one face is decoration.

Fill fastener holes, sand to 120, and stain or paint. Slide it against the sofa arm and put the remote on it before someone loses the remote again.
materials: Wood filler, sandpaper, and stain or paint

images.alt: Slim waterfall-style wooden side table beside a sofa arm
